fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: New York Certified Dietitian-Nutritionist (CDN) Application — Requirements & Fees (2026)
seo.description: Apply for a New York Certified Dietitian-Nutritionist (CDN) credential through the NYSED Office of the Professions. $294 total estimated fee. Complete requirements, exam info, and official links.
seo.keywords: New York dietitian license, NY CDN certification, NYSED dietitian application, New York nutritionist requirements, dietetics board NY, cost of dietitian license NY

board.name: New York State Board for Dietetics and Nutrition
board.mailing_address:
NYS Education Department
Office of the Professions
Registration Unit
State Education Building, 2nd Floor
Albany, NY 12234-1000

license_types.title: Certified Dietitian-Nutritionist (CDN)
license_types.description: The standard credential for practicing dietetics and nutrition in New York State. Demands completion of a Master's degree, ACEND-approved supervised practice, and passing the national CDR exam.

steps.title: Complete Education & Exam Requirements
steps.description: Earn a Master's degree, complete at least 1,000 hours of ACEND-accredited supervised practice, and pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) national examination.

steps.title: Take the Child Abuse Identification Course
steps.description: New York State requires all healthcare professionals to complete state-approved coursework in identifying and reporting child abuse before applying for certification.

steps.title: Apply Online via NYSED
steps.description: Submit your application for a new CDN certification through the Office of the Professions online portal and pay the combined $294 application and initial registration fee.

documents.detail: Must be sent directly from your degree-granting university to the NYSED.
Proof of active registration sent directly from the Commission on Dietetic Registration.
Certificate of completion for the mandatory NYS child abuse identification coursework.

provisional_text: New York State does not issue temporary permits or provisional licenses for dietitians or nutritionists.
reciprocity_text: New York does not maintain automatic reciprocity agreements with other states. Out-of-state applicants must apply for a new license by equivalence, providing direct verification of their out-of-state license, CDR registration, and proof they meet all New York education and experience standards.

faqs.question: How much does a New York dietitian license cost?
faqs.answer: The total initial cost is $294, which covers the NYSED's $115 application fee and the $179 fee for your first three-year registration period.

faqs.question: Do I need a master's degree to become a CDN in New York?
faqs.answer: Yes. As of January 1, 2024, a minimum of a master's degree is required by the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) to sit for the national RDN exam, which serves as the exam basis for NY state certification.

faqs.question: Is continuing education required to renew a New York CDN license?
faqs.answer: The NYSED does not mandate additional state-level continuing education for renewal, but you must maintain your active CDR registration, which requires completing 75 hours of continuing professional education every 5 years.
